claim: Sixteenth MCC measurement: v13 composed detector + 30 new validated annotation classes (protein_deformylation, translation_initiation_formyltransferase, methionine_aminopeptidase, signal_recognition_particle, methionine_adenosyltransferase, nad_biosynthesis, dna_ligase, glycolysis_gapdh, ribosome_gtpase, trna_pseudouridine_synthase, ctp_synthase, excinuclease, rrna_maturation, ribosome_binding_factor, acp_synthase, trna_uridine_carboxymethylaminomethyl, membrane_protein_insertase, iron_sulfur_cluster, cysteine_desulfurase, thioredoxin_reductase, adenine_salvage_prt, uracil_salvage_prt, aaa_protease, clp_protease, ribonuclease_hi, ribonuclease_m5, formyltetrahydrofolate_cyclo_ligase, ribose_5_phosphate_isomerase, phosphatidylglycerol_synthase, glycolipid_synthase); dna_replication_core broadened ('dna polymerase iii' -> 'dna polymerase' to catch polA); iMB155 patches updated to fire at 20 tokens per step (fixes the v12 NNATr regression). Full 455-gene sweep at seed=42: MCC = 0.494, delta +0.084 over v13's 0.410, delta +0.130 over v10b's 0.364.
source_detail: Reproducible via 'python scripts/run_sweep_parallel.py --all --workers 4 --use-rust --scale 0.05 --t-end-s 0.5 --detector composed --seed 42 --threshold 0.1 --enable-imb155-patches'. The 30 new annotation classes + broadened dna_replication_core live in cell_sim/layer6_essentiality/annotation_class_detector.py; the 20-token iMB155 patch fix in cell_sim/layer3_reactions/imb155_patches.py::_UNGATED_TOKEN_COUNT. Outputs preserved at outputs/predictions_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_composed_all455_v14_v14priors_nnatr.csv + outputs/metrics_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_composed_all455_v14_v14priors_nnatr.json. Sweep wall: 3009.9 s (6.6 s/gene effective on 4 workers; slower than v13 because the 20-token patched rules fire more events per sim step).

nnatr_recovery.v13_state: FN (wt=14 below min_wt_events=20 threshold — v12 NNATr regression)
nnatr_recovery.fix: imb155_patches _UNGATED_TOKEN_COUNT = 20 instead of 1; restores effective propensity of patched rules to match a typical Mycoplasma enzyme pool

caveats: Item-2 pivot prediction accuracy: pre-sweep expected TP +39 FP +0 MCC ~0.494. Actual: TP +39 FP +0 MCC 0.494. Exact.

Item-2 NNATr regression fix succeeded. JCVISYN3A_0380 went FN -> TP as predicted. Evidence string shows NNATr wt_count 14 (v13) -> 24 (v14), crossing the PerRuleDetector min_wt_events=20 threshold. No other catalysis rule dropped below the threshold in v14; WT dynamics are otherwise comparable to v10b (precision 0.987 -> 0.989).

Recall 0.705 still below the brief target of 0.59 MCC but getting closer on MCC itself. Remaining 113 FNs split: ~84 uncharacterised / putative / hypothetical proteins with no annotation signal and no catalytic rule (require Tier-1 ML features or Path A longer-window sims); ~29 miscellaneous specifically-named genes (e.g. hupA DNA-binding, nusB antitermination, ftsY membrane insertase) with narrow annotations not yet in the keyword KB.

Per-gene wall time rose from v13's 2.2 s/gene to 6.6 s/gene because the 20-token patched rules fire 20x more events per sim step. Full-sweep wall went 999s -> 3010s. Acceptable for a one-shot ground-truth measurement; if per-gene throughput matters for a future multi-seed sweep, reducing _UNGATED_TOKEN_COUNT to 10 likely keeps MCC stable while halving the sim cost.

Session 15 cumulative result so far: MCC 0.364 -> 0.494, +0.130 absolute, +35.7 percent relative. All lift from correctly-diagnosed simulator-biology (iMB155 patches) + annotation-KB extensions (tRNA-modification + 30 new priors). Zero detector-design changes. The v10b baseline was not the simulator-MCC ceiling; it was the ceiling of a partial annotation KB + a mis-over-assigned placeholder locus.

Next honest lever: (a) Tier-1 XGB with populated esmfold_v1 + mace_off_kcat parquets (Session-15 items 0a, 0b pending Colab run); (b) add ComplexAssembly entries for tsaBCDE and gatABC heteromers (currently caught by keyword priors, tech-debt); (c) mine the 84 uncharacterised FNs via structural/coevolutionary signals.
